feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService

entry.title: Severe Thunderstorm Watch
entry.summary:
*Updated at 8:30pm to lift the Watch for inland Gisborne and Hawke's Bay, southern Taupo, Taihape, Waitomo and Taumarunui.*
A showery unstable air mass continues to affect the North Island this evening. For inland Bay of Plenty (including Rotorua), northern Taupo, and southeastern Waikato there is a moderate risk of severe thunderstorms with localised downpours bringing rainfall intensities of 25 to 40 mm/h.
Rainfall of this intensity can cause surface and/or flash flooding, especially about low-lying areas such as streams, rivers or narrow valleys, and may also lead to slips. Driving conditions will also be hazardous with surface flooding and poor visibility in heavy rain.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
